channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: An expert on Iranian politics reviews the status of negotiations to end the war on Iran
item.description: NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to Mehrzad Boroujerdi of the Missouri University of Science and Technology about the status of the Trump Administration's negotiations to end the war on Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to Mehrzad Boroujerdi of the Missouri University of Science and Technology about the status of the Trump Administration's negotiations to end the war on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Why saying hello to strangers can be good for you
item.description: A study establishes that "social ties" — a fancy way of saying being nice to other, even those you don't know — has benefits. A teacher asked her students to test the thesis in real life.
item.content:encoded: A study establishes that "social ties" — a fancy way of saying being nice to other, even those you don't know — has benefits. A teacher asked her students to test the thesis in real life.
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Southern Republicans redistrict after Supreme Court rules, Dems lose big in Virginia
item.description: The Supreme Court weakened minority voting rights and prompted Republicans in four states to move to redistrict as part of Trump's push. A court nullified Democratic redistricting in Virginia.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court weakened minority voting rights and prompted Republicans in four states to move to redistrict as part of Trump's push. A court nullified Democratic redistricting in Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Larry Kaplow

item.title: How the 1994 World Cup kicked off America's love affair with soccer
item.description: Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?
item.content:encoded:
Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?

(Image credit: Lois Bernstein)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: They graduate to six figure salaries, and grueling work
item.description: Cadets from the nation's Merchant Marine academies are finding lots of demand and great salaries because of a shortage of licensed mariners.
item.content:encoded:
Cadets from the nation's Merchant Marine academies are finding lots of demand and great salaries because of a shortage of licensed mariners.

(Image credit: SUNY Maritime College)
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Moscow marks Victory Day with a Red Square parade under tight security
item.description: Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, even as a U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.
item.content:encoded:
Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, even as a U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.

(Image credit: Pavel Bednyakov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What is a radical? It's the question of M.I.A.'s vexing career
item.description: Fans who danced to "Paper Planes" might hardly recognize the conspiracy-touting artist before them today — but in a certain way, she's the same button-pusher as ever.
item.content:encoded:
Fans who danced to "Paper Planes" might hardly recognize the conspiracy-touting artist before them today — but in a certain way, she's the same button-pusher as ever.

(Image credit: Rick Kern)
item.dc:creator: Sheldon Pearce

item.title: National mood is against Republicans, but redistricting could help prop them up
item.description: The national political landscape looks bad for President Trump and Republicans, but recent wins in the redistricting fight could soften the blow they might have suffered without them.
item.content:encoded:
The national political landscape looks bad for President Trump and Republicans, but recent wins in the redistricting fight could soften the blow they might have suffered without them.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: ABC argues Trump administration is trying to chill free speech
item.description: In a filing, ABC accuses the Trump administration of trying to chill its constitutionally protected free speech. The point of contention: "The View," and whether it's subject to equal time rules.
item.content:encoded:
In a filing, ABC accuses the Trump administration of trying to chill its constitutionally protected free speech. The point of contention: "The View," and whether it's subject to equal time rules.

(Image credit: Gabriela Passos)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a 3-day ceasefire
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Yuri Ushakov, President Vladimir Putin's foreign affairs adviser, both confirmed the agreement for a three-day ceasefire and an exchange of prisoners.
item.content:encoded:
President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Yuri Ushakov, President Vladimir Putin's foreign affairs adviser, both confirmed the agreement for a three-day ceasefire and an exchange of prisoners.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
